steps.title: Find the slope of line j using perpendicularity
steps.content:
Line $k$ has equation $x + y = 0$. Rewriting in slope-intercept form: $y = -x$, so the slope is $-1$.

For perpendicular lines, slopes are negative reciprocals. The slope of line $j$ is:
$$-\frac{1}{-1} = 1$$

steps.title: Write the equation of line j
steps.content:
Line $j$ has slope \$1$ and passes through $(0, 3)$.

Using slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$:
$$y = x + 3$$

Rearranging to standard form:
$$x - y = -3$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
